violations.text: The door to the employee toilet room is not self-closing. New Violation. Correct By: 02/16/2026

The floor and wall junctures in the following areas do not have coving. 1. employee restroom doorframe 2. warewashing area floor 3. cookline wall/floor (exposed insulation) The floor and wall junctures must be coved and closed to no larger than 1/32nd of an inch Repeat Violation. Correct By: 02/16/2026
The ceiling in the warewashing area is covered with tiles that are not smooth, durable, and easily cleanable. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 02/16/2026
Missing ceiling tiles were observed in the following areas: 1. employee restroom 2. above walk-in cooler Repeat Violation. Correct By: 02/16/2026

The walls behind hand sink are constructed of wood. This material is not durable. Materials for wall surfaces shall be smooth, durable, easily cleanable, and non-absorbent for areas subject to moisture such as food preparation areas, walk-in refrigerators, ware-washing areas, and toilet rooms. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 02/16/2026

Garlic was observed held at 53 degrees Fahrenheit. Time/temperature control for safety (TCS) food must be held at 41 degrees Fahrenheit or below except during preparation, cooking, or cooling. PIC moved to prep unit at time of inspection. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 02/04/2026

Proper cooling methods were not used for time/temperature control for safety (TCS) foods. At time of inspection, sauce was placed hot in the refrigerator in covered containers greater than four inches deep. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 02/04/2026

At the time of inspection, ROP salmon was thawing with package intact. Reduced oxygen packaging fish that bears a label indicating that it is to be kept frozen until time of use shall be removed from the reduced oxygen environment. Temperature was observed to be at 41 degrees Fahrenheit; PIC voluntarily disposed of. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 02/04/2026
The ceiling in the warewashing area is covered with tiles that are not smooth, durable, and easily cleanable. New Violation. Correct By: 03/04/2026

The floor and wall junctures in the following areas do not have coving. 1. employee restroom doorframe 2. warewashing area floor 3. cookline wall/floor (exposed insulation) The floor and wall junctures must be coved and closed to no larger than 1/32nd of an inch. New Violation. Correct By: 02/18/2026
Missing ceiling tiles were observed in the following areas: 1. employee restroom 2. above walk-in cooler New Violation. Correct By: 02/18/2026

The walls behind hand sink are constructed of wood. This material is not durable. Materials for wall surfaces shall be smooth, durable, easily cleanable, and non-absorbent for areas subject to moisture such as food preparation areas, walk-in refrigerators, ware-washing areas, and toilet rooms. New Violation. Correct By: 02/18/2026

Staff were observed not wearing hair/beard restraints. Food employees must wear hair restraints such as hats, hair coverings, or nets that are worn to effectively keep their hair from contacting exposed food, clean equipment/utensils, linens, and unwrapped single-service and single-use articles. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 03/06/2024

Wet wiping cloths are not stored in a chemical sanitizer between uses. Wet wiping cloths must be stored in a chemical sanitizer at the appropriate concentration. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 03/06/2024
The facility is lacking test strips to measure the concentration of the quaternary ammonium sanitizing solution for the three bay sink. New Violation. Correct By: 03/13/2024
The door to the employee restroom is not self-closing. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 03/13/2024
